speed_limits.notes: Posted signs override defaults. Lower limits can apply in tunnels, roadworks, residential zones, and environmental speed-control areas.

alcohol_limit.notes: A lower 0.01% BAC limit applies to novice drivers and some professional drivers.

rules.seatbelt_rules: Seatbelts are mandatory for all occupants.
rules.child_seat_rules: Children under 14 years and shorter than 135 cm need an appropriate child restraint.
rules.phone_usage_rules: Handheld phone use is prohibited while driving. Hands-free systems are allowed.
rules.headlights_rules: Use headlights at night, in tunnels, and when visibility is poor. Motorcycles must use lights during the day.
rules.priority_rules: Drive on the right. Right-before-left applies at unmarked intersections unless signs or road markings say otherwise.

tolls.notes: Motorways and expressways require a valid vignette or digital toll. Some Alpine tunnels and passes have additional special tolls.

rules.parking_rules: Short-stay parking zones are common in cities and are strictly enforced. Check local signs and digital parking rules.
rules.winter_rules: From 1 November to 15 April, winter tyres are required in winter road conditions. Snow chains may be required where signed.

summary: Austria is generally straightforward for road trips, but motorways require a vignette and mountain weather can change driving conditions quickly. Expect strict speed, parking, and winter-equipment enforcement, especially around cities and Alpine routes.
common_traps: Driving on motorways or expressways without a valid vignette or digital toll.
Special toll roads in Alpine tunnels and passes are separate from the normal vignette.
Winter tyres are required in winter road conditions from 1 November to 15 April.
City parking zones can be confusing and are enforced quickly.

faq.question: Do I need a vignette in Austria?
faq.question_de: Brauche ich eine Vignette in Österreich?
faq.answer: Yes. Motorways (Autobahn) and expressways (Schnellstraße) require a valid vignette or digital toll registration before you drive on them. Driving without one is enforced with fines and can affect rental vehicles.
faq.answer_de: Ja. Autobahnen und Schnellstraßen erfordern eine gültige Vignette oder digitale Mautregistrierung, bevor du sie benutzt. Fahren ohne Vignette wird geahndet und kann Mietwagen betreffen.

faq.question: Can I buy a digital vignette instead of a sticker?
faq.question_de: Kann ich eine digitale Vignette statt eines Klebevignettes kaufen?
faq.answer: Yes. Austria offers digital vignettes linked to your licence plate through ASFINAG. Buy before entering tolled roads. Keep proof of purchase and confirm the plate number is correct.
faq.answer_de: Ja. Österreich bietet digitale Vignetten, die über ASFINAG mit dem Kennzeichen verknüpft werden. Kaufe sie vor der Nutzung mautpflichtiger Straßen und prüfe das Kennzeichen.

faq.question: Are there toll roads beyond the standard vignette?
faq.question_de: Gibt es Mautstraßen zusätzlich zur Standard-Vignette?
faq.answer: Yes. Some Alpine tunnels and special routes require separate tolls in addition to the vignette. Tyrol and Salzburg routes are common examples—check your route before you travel.
faq.answer_de: Ja. Einige Alpentunnel und Sonderstrecken erfordern zusätzliche Maut neben der Vignette—besonders in Tirol und Salzburg. Route vorab prüfen.

faq.question: Can I use my EU driving licence in Austria?
faq.question_de: Kann ich meinen EU-Führerschein in Österreich nutzen?
faq.answer: EU and EEA driving licences are generally accepted for tourists. Carry your physical licence and passport or ID. Long-term residents may need to exchange their licence under Austrian rules.
faq.answer_de: EU- und EWR-Führerscheine werden für Touristen in der Regel akzeptiert. Führerschein und Ausweis mitführen. Langzeitaufenthalter müssen ggf. umschreiben.

faq.question: Do I need an International Driving Permit (IDP)?
faq.answer: Non-EU licences may require an IDP depending on your country of issue and rental company policy. Obtain the IDP in your home country before travel—it cannot be issued in Austria.
faq.answer_de: Nicht-EU-Führerscheine können je nach Herkunftsland und Mietwagenanbieter einen IDP erfordern. IDP vor der Reise im Heimatland besorgen.

faq.question: What equipment must I carry in the car?
faq.question_de: Welche Ausrüstung muss im Auto mitgeführt werden?
faq.answer: Required items typically include a warning triangle, high-visibility vest, and first-aid kit. Check the latest official list before driving, especially in a rental car.
faq.answer_de: Pflichtausstattung umfasst typischerweise Warndreieck, Warnweste und Verbandskasten. Offizielle Liste vor der Fahrt prüfen, besonders bei Mietwagen.

faq.question: What are the standard speed limits in Austria?
faq.answer: Unless signed otherwise, typical limits are 50 km/h in urban areas, 100 km/h on rural roads, and 130 km/h on motorways. Always follow posted signs—they override general limits.
faq.answer_de: Typische Limits: 50 km/h innerorts, 100 km/h außerorts, 130 km/h auf Autobahnen—sofern nicht anders ausgeschildert. Schilder haben Vorrang.

faq.question: How strict is speed enforcement in Austria?
faq.question_de: Wie streng wird Tempo in Österreich kontrolliert?
faq.answer: Speed enforcement is strict and widespread, including fixed cameras, mobile units, and section control. Even small excesses outside urban areas can result in fines.
faq.answer_de: Tempokontrollen sind streng und weit verbreitet—stationär, mobil und Streckenmessung. Schon kleine Überschreitungen außerorts können Bußgelder nach sich ziehen.

faq.question: What is the fine for using a phone while driving?
faq.question_de: Welches Bußgeld droht für Handy am Steuer?
faq.answer: Using a handheld phone while driving is prohibited. Fines apply on the spot or by mail. Use hands-free systems only when permitted and keep full attention on the road.
faq.answer_de: Handygebrauch am Steuer ist verboten. Bußgelder werden vor Ort oder per Post verhängt. Freisprechanlage nur nutzen, wenn erlaubt.

faq.question: What is the alcohol limit for drivers in Austria?
faq.question_de: Wie hoch ist die Promillegrenze in Österreich?
faq.answer: The general limit for experienced drivers is 0.5‰ blood alcohol. Lower limits apply to novice and professional drivers. Penalties increase quickly above the limit.
faq.answer_de: Für erfahrene Fahrer gilt in der Regel 0,5‰. Für Fahranfänger und Berufsfahrer gelten niedrigere Grenzen. Strafen steigen schnell bei Überschreitung.

faq.question: Are winter tyres required in Austria?
faq.answer: From 1 November to 15 April, winter tyres are required whenever winter road conditions apply—not only when snow is visible. All-season tyres must meet the M+S marking requirements.
faq.answer_de: Vom 1. November bis 15. April sind Winterreifen bei winterlichen Straßenverhältnissen Pflicht—notfalls auch ohne sichtbaren Schnee. Ganzjahresreifen brauchen M+S-Kennzeichnung.

faq.question: When are snow chains required?
faq.answer: Snow chains may be mandatory on signed Alpine routes during winter conditions, even if winter tyres are fitted. Carry chains when driving mountain passes, especially in Tyrol.
faq.answer_de: Schneeketten können auf ausgeschilderten Alpenstrecken bei Winterbedingungen Pflicht sein—auch mit Winterreifen. Ketten mitführen, besonders in Tirol.

faq.question: What rules apply to motorcycles and mopeds?
faq.question_de: Welche Regeln gelten für Motorrad und Moped?
faq.answer: Helmets are mandatory for riders and passengers. Motorcycles follow the same speed and alcohol rules as cars on most roads. Lane splitting is not permitted. Check vehicle-specific tabs on the country guide.
faq.answer_de: Helmpflicht für Fahrer und Beifahrer. Motorräder unterliegen meist denselben Tempo- und Alkoholregeln wie Autos. Überholen zwischen Spuren ist nicht erlaubt.

faq.question: What should I know when renting a car in Austria?
faq.question_de: Was sollte ich beim Mietwagen in Österreich beachten?
faq.answer: Confirm vignette status with the rental company, inspect mandatory equipment, and understand cross-border policies. Non-EU drivers should verify IDP requirements before pickup.
faq.answer_de: Vignettenstatus, Pflichtausstattung und Grenzübertrittsregeln mit dem Vermieter klären. Nicht-EU-Fahrer sollten IDP-Anforderungen vor Abholung prüfen.

rental_and_idp_notes: EU/EEA licences are generally accepted. Non-EU drivers should check rental-company requirements; an International Driving Permit is recommended and may be required depending on the licence.

regional_variations.notes: Short-stay parking applies citywide in many areas. Use official signs, parking apps, or public transport where possible.

highlights.title: Short-term parking zones
highlights.description: Short-stay parking applies citywide in many areas. Use official signs, parking apps, or public transport where possible.

highlights.title: Environmental zones
highlights.description: Some districts restrict older or high-emission vehicles. Check local signage and official city transport pages before driving in.

regional_variations.notes: Mountain passes, snow chains, seasonal closures, and special toll roads are common. Check route conditions before driving.

highlights.title: Alpine passes and closures
highlights.description: Mountain passes may close seasonally or during heavy snow. Check ASFINAG and local route information before you travel.

highlights.title: Snow chains
highlights.description: Snow chains can be required on signed Alpine routes in winter conditions, even if winter tyres are fitted.

highlights.title: Special toll roads
highlights.description: Some tunnels and passes require a separate toll in addition to the standard vignette.

regional_variations.notes: Historic city centre parking is limited and strictly enforced.

highlights.title: Historic city parking
highlights.description: Parking in the old town is limited and enforced quickly. Use park-and-ride options or official parking garages where possible.
